questions.question: Why does the `useFetch` composable accept `MaybeRefOrGetter<string>` instead of a plain string?
options: To support optional URLs that may be undefined at mount time
So the URL can be a plain string, a ref, or a getter, enabling automatic re-fetch on URL change
To allow the URL to be computed from a Pinia store action
Because TypeScript requires this type for all async parameters

questions.question: Why is `watchEffect` used inside the basic `useFetch` composable instead of a plain function call?
options: Because async functions cannot be called directly in `<script setup>`
Because it runs immediately AND re-runs automatically when reactive URL dependencies change
Because it provides built-in error handling for network requests
Because `watch` requires an explicit dependency array to track URL changes

questions.question: What does the `refetch` property exposed by the composable allow the caller to do?
options: Change the URL used for the next request
Cancel an in-flight request before it completes
Manually trigger a new fetch, for example on user-initiated retry
